description: Effect-link graph: 'change A => must review/change B'. Seeded with architecture invariants; grows as code lands (see protocols/effect-link.md).

to.ref: @tessera/storage:adapters/* (sqlite, sqlite-vec, filesystem, in-proc; later postgres, pgvector, s3, bullmq)

links.rationale: A storage port is implemented by every adapter and validated by one shared conformance suite; changing the interface breaks all adapters and the suite, and a semantic change needs an ADR.
The KG/effect-link model feeds graph retrieval, get_effects, and the dashboard; the harness effects schema mirrors the product model, so changing one should be reflected in the others.

to.ref: @tessera/api route Zod schemas (apps/api/src/schemas/*) — the single source for validation + serialization + OpenAPI

@tessera/mcp tools (apps/mcp/src/server.ts: search/compile_context/get_effects/capture_memory/explain) — wrap the SAME domain services as REST via a type-only ApiServices import (F-012, one engine/two surfaces)

links.rationale: BOTH surfaces now realized. REST (F-011): @tessera/api route Zod schemas (zod/v4, fastify-type-provider-zod) are the single source for validation + serialization + OpenAPI (GET /v1/openapi.json). MCP (F-012): @tessera/mcp tools wrap the SAME domain services (type-only ApiServices import, so no Fastify in the MCP runtime) with the same masked error-envelope policy. Changing a shared domain service, the error-envelope shape, or a route/tool contract must be reflected across REST + MCP + the regenerated OpenAPI + the SDK + the dashboard — additive/versioned (NFR-11). Both surfaces take injected services; deployment-profile wiring + the bootable processes are F-015.

from.ref: docs/design/DESIGN-SYSTEM.md design tokens (semantic CSS variables / theme)

links.rationale: Components consume design tokens only; changing a token or the theme contract affects every component, the chart palette, and both themes, so token changes must be reviewed across the UI.
CI runs the verification gates; the workflow steps and the verification protocol table must mirror gates.json. Adding/removing/renaming a gate requires updating the CI workflow and the protocol.

to.ref: every @tessera/* package and app (all depend on @tessera/core)

links.rationale: @tessera/core is the dependency-free base every package imports; changing/removing an exported member (Id, errors, Result, config types, EventBus) can break any consumer. Change additively and review dependents.

to.ref: @tessera/storage adapters (sqlite-relational, filesystem-blob, in-process-queue, sqlite-vec; later postgres, pgvector, s3, bullmq)

links.rationale: Each storage port is implemented by every adapter and validated by a shared conformance suite; changing a port interface breaks all adapters and their suites (concrete instance of E-001 for @tessera/storage).

to.ref: @tessera/ai adapters (transformers, ollama, fake; later hosted providers)

links.rationale: Changing the Embeddings port affects every adapter, its conformance suite, and consumers; changing the embedding model/dimension also requires re-embedding stored vectors (ADR-0006).

to.ref: @tessera/ingestion connectors (filesystem, git; later github, etc.) + processors (normalize, redact; later chunk/extract/embed)
@tessera/ingestion pipeline (coordinator + worker) — enqueue/consume via the @tessera/storage Queue port

links.rationale: The ingestion contracts are the plugin SDK for sources/stages and the seam to persistence: changing Connector/Processor breaks the first-party connectors/processors + the conformance suite; changing DocumentSink/IngestionManifest breaks the worker and every downstream sink that persists documents. Redaction (FR-9) is an enforced terminal stage — never relax it.

to.ref: @tessera/memory adapters (in-memory, sqlite via storage Drizzle handle)
sqlite `memories` table DDL in the sqlite adapter (kept in lockstep with the Drizzle schema; migration tooling F-024)

links.rationale: MemoryStore is implemented by every adapter and validated by one conformance suite (E-001 instance); the Memory version model (immutable versions + supersede pointers, FR-12) is the contract the service and all consumers rely on. Changing the port/model breaks adapters + suite + service; changing the sqlite DDL must track the Drizzle schema. The never-silently-mutated invariant must hold across adapters.

to.ref: @tessera/knowledge-graph effects (static-derivation, ranking — shared by both adapters for parity) + service

links.rationale: Concrete instance of E-002: GraphStore is implemented by every adapter and validated by one conformance suite; the shared ranking/static-derivation make the two adapters' get_effects identical (change one → re-verify both). The effect-link model mirrors the harness effects.schema (E-002). Node-population from code (symbol extraction) is a downstream ingestion concern, not part of this contract.

from.ref: @tessera/retrieval Retriever interface + Candidate model + fusion ranker (fuse)

to.ref: @tessera/retrieval retrievers (semantic→VectorStore, keyword→FTS5, graph→GraphStore, symbolic→GraphStore) + hybrid service

links.rationale: The Retriever interface + Candidate shape + fusion contract are what the compiler's retrieve stage and the API/MCP search build on; changing them breaks every retriever, the conformance suite, and consumers. Adding the temporal retriever (FR-24, F-018) must fit this same interface. Meaningful fusion needs a consistent `ref` space across backends — established by ingestion/config, not retrieval.

to.ref: @tessera/context-compiler stage unit tests + compiler/beats-naive integration tests
consumers: @tessera/api + @tessera/mcp compile_context (F-011/F-012), @tessera/web Package Inspector (F-014) renders the trace/provenance
depends on: @tessera/retrieval (E-012), @tessera/knowledge-graph get_effects (E-011); corpus via the FragmentSource seam (ingestion)

links.rationale: The ContextPackage shape (sections/provenance/whyIncluded), the CompilationTrace, and the CompileRequest are the contract REST/MCP `compile_context` returns and the web Package Inspector renders (concretely advances E-003); changing them ripples to API/MCP/web. The compiler consumes the retrieval + graph contracts. CQS (PRD §9) is the regression guard that compilation beats naive top-k. FragmentSource is the corpus seam ingestion fills.

from.ref: @tessera/config config schema (TesseraConfig) + Local profile composition (createLocalRuntime) + SecretsProvider port

to.ref: @tessera/config Local profile wiring (profiles/local.ts) — constructs every storage/ai/domain adapter and composes ApiServices

@tessera/config blob-backed FragmentSource (fragment-source.ts) — the compiler's corpus seam over the filesystem BlobStore; ingestion's persistent DocumentSink must write the same {kind,text,metadata} convention
@tessera/config secrets adapters (env, file) implementing the SecretsProvider port (KMS/vault for cloud)
consumers: the runnable REST/MCP process bins that boot from a Runtime; type-only on @tessera/api ApiServices (E-003)
@tessera/storage Postgres+pgvector adapters (F-023) add a self-hosted/cloud profile branch behind the same Runtime/config — no domain change

links.rationale: @tessera/config is the composition root: the config schema + Local profile select and wire every adapter (sqlite/sqlite-vec/filesystem/in-process/transformers) into the ApiServices both surfaces consume. Changing the config schema, the profile wiring, the SecretsProvider port, or the blob FragmentSource convention ripples to whoever boots a runtime and to the future Postgres/cloud profile (F-023). The embedding dimension flows from the provider to the vector store (ADR-0006). ApiServices is imported type-only so api never depends on config (no cycle).

to.ref: @tessera/server bins/startApiServer/startMcpServer — wire the logger, instrumentServices, the http-latency hook, and env-gated startTelemetry
@tessera/api buildServer loggerInstance option (additive) — the redacting Pino logger backs per-request logging + correlation (additive on E-003)
@tessera/context-compiler TraceStage.durationMs (additive on E-013) — feeds recordCompileStageDurations (compile latency per stage)
seams (instrument provided, not yet fed): per-adapter spans; registerQueueDepthGauge needs a Queue depth source; OTLP exporters via config

links.rationale: Observability is cross-cutting but kept additive: libraries use the OTel API only (no-op until startTelemetry registers the SDK at the process), instrumentServices wraps ApiServices without touching domain packages, and redaction keeps secrets/content out of logs (NFR-7). Changing the instruments, the span/log contract, or the redaction policy affects the server wiring and any future surface that boots a runtime. The compiler's durationMs and buildServer's loggerInstance are additive — the surfaces' response schemas ignore the new optional trace field. Deepening to per-adapter spans / a fed queue-depth gauge must preserve these seams.

to.ref: @tessera/plugin-host first-party plugins (filesystem connector, fake/transformers embeddings) — wrap the existing factories on the same contract

the existing extension-point ports the PluginKinds map to: Connector/Processor (@tessera/ingestion E-009), Embeddings (@tessera/ai E-008), storage ports (@tessera/storage E-007), Retriever (@tessera/retrieval E-012)
third-party plugins import the SDK; the deployment profile (F-015) may later wire adapters via the host

links.rationale: The Plugin SDK is a uniform ENVELOPE over the existing ports — it does not re-define them. Changing the Plugin/Manifest/Instance contract or the host (load/lifecycle/isolation semantics) breaks every first-party + third-party plugin and the conformance tests; the PluginKinds must stay aligned with the underlying ports (E-007/8/9/12). First-party wrappers live in plugin-host so it depends on ingestion/ai one-way (no cycle); domain packages are untouched. Failure isolation (config-invalid + setup/lifecycle errors → failed, never crash the host) is the FR-58 invariant.
